up.method: headspace GC (TCD); ~800 mmol/L headspace reduced to <0.8 mmol/L headspace over 39 h; (800-0.8)/39 = 20.49 mmol/L headspace/h volumetric average, NOT per gDW

sec.method: headspace GC (TCD); final CH4 = 208 mmol/L headspace reached after 39 h; 208/39 = 5.33 mmol/L headspace/h volumetric average, NOT per gDW

product_yields.CH4_per_H2_stoichiometric: 1 CH4 per 4 H2 (4H2+CO2=CH4+2H2O); final 208 mmol/L CH4 consistent with ~800 mmol/L H2 consumed

growth_records.cite: Enoki et al. Comparative Proteomic Analysis of Methanothermobacter themautotrophicus ΔH in Pure Culture and in Co-Culture with a Butyrate-Oxidizing Bacterium. PLoS ONE (2011). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0024309. [PMC3164167]
growth_records.snippet: the doubling time of M. thermautotrophicus estimated from methane formation was approximately 2.8 h... The approximately 800 mmol l-1 headspace of initial H2 was reduced to below 0.8 mmol l-1 headspace after 39 h of cultivation, and the fin
growth_records.notes: PURE CULTURE (reference condition). Doubling time td=2.8 h reported (from methane formation). mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/2.8 = 0.2476 /h. H2 uptake volumetric average = (800 - 0.8)/39 h = 20.49 mmol/L headspace/h (concentrations and 39 h duration explicitly stated; includes ~10 h lag so true log-phase rate is higher). CH4 secretion volumetric average = 208 mmol/L headspace / 39 h = 5.33 mmol/L headspace/h (final CH4 and 39 h explicitly stated). No biomass (gDW) reported so rates cannot be converted to mmol/gDW/h. Stoichiometry stated: 4H2+CO2=CH4+2H2O. Temperature 55 C, anaerobic, stirred 700 rpm.

growth_records.medium: minimal salts medium (reduced) + 40 mM sodium butyrate, N2-CO2 atmosphere

up.method: headspace GC; only trace H2 (<43 umol/L headspace) detected during butyrate oxidation because it is immediately scavenged by the methanogen (interspecies H2 transfer); steady-state pool not a rate

sec.method: HPLC/GC; coculture converted 32 mM butyrate to 14 mM CH4 over the ~8-day (192 h) butyrate-oxidation window; 14 mM/192 h = 0.073 mmol/L/h volumetric average, NOT per gDW

product_yields.CH4_per_butyrate: 14 mM CH4 / 32 mM butyrate = 0.44 mol CH4/mol butyrate (overall 2 butyrate + CO2 + 2 H2O = 4 acetate + CH4 gives 0.5 theoretical)

growth_records.cite: Enoki et al. Comparative Proteomic Analysis of Methanothermobacter themautotrophicus ΔH in Pure Culture and in Co-Culture with a Butyrate-Oxidizing Bacterium. PLoS ONE (2011). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0024309. [PMC3164167]
growth_records.snippet: this coculture finally, almost stoichiometrically, converted 32 mM of butyrate to 59 mM of acetate and 14 mM of methane (2CH3CH2CH2COOH+CO2+2H2O = 4CH3COOH+CH4). Only a trace amount of H2 (<43 umol l-1 headspace) was detected... The mean ge
growth_records.notes: CO-CULTURE (methanogen partner). Mean generation time td=19 h reported (log phase, from methane formation); 6.8x slower than pure culture (19/2.8=6.79). mu = ln2/19 = 0.693147/19 = 0.0365 /h. Methanogen substrate = H2+CO2 from syntroph; H2 stays trace (<43 umol/L headspace). CH4 production rate = 14 mM / 192 h = 0.073 mmol/L/h (14 mM CH4 explicit; 192 h = 8-day butyrate-oxidation window explicit). No biomass reported; volumetric only. Temperature 55 C, anaerobic, unstirred.

up.method: gas supplied 25-50 mL/min; per-biomass consumption only in Supplemental Fig. 3, not numerically in text

sec.method: HPLC; steady-state volumetric production rate at 42 C (titer 0.031 mM); peak 1.80 umol/L/h on day 8
off-gas mass spectrometry; only in Supplemental Fig. 3, not numerically in text

growth_records.cite: Baur, Tina et al. Metabolic engineering of Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus DeltaH for recombinant acetoin production. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00275. [PMC13049647]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultivation in a chemostat resulted in stable acetoin production rates of 1.28 +/- 0.07 umol L-1 h-1 (0.11 +/- 0.01 mg L-1 h-1) at 42 C ... hydraulic retention time (HRT) of 1 day ... medium inflow rate of 83.3 mL h-1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, 2 L, HRT 1 day so D = 1/24 h = 0.0417 h-1; independently, inflow 83.3 mL/h / 2000 mL = 0.0417 h-1 (agrees). At steady state mu = D = 0.0417 h-1 (calculated). doubling_time = ln2/0.0417 = 16.62 h. MEASURED acetoin secretion 1.28 +/- 0.07 umol/L/h at 42 C. Substrate H2/CO2 and product CH4 rates were monitored (off-gas MS) but quantified only in supplemental figures, not in main text. MS medium composition referenced to Casini et al. 2023 / Fink et al. 2021, not stated in full here.

up.method: gas sparged 25-50 mL/min; per-biomass rate only in supplemental figure

sec.method: HPLC; final average specific acetoin titer normalized to OD600 across fed-batch bioreactors
off-gas MS; normalized cumulative methane in Fig. 4B, not numerically in text

growth_records.cite: Baur, Tina et al. Metabolic engineering of Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus DeltaH for recombinant acetoin production. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00275. [PMC13049647]
growth_records.snippet: The final average specific acetoin titer (normalized to OD600) in the fed-batch bioreactors was 0.296 +/- 0.063 mmol L-1 OD600-1 (Table 4)
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch 2 L bioreactor, 42 C, pH 7.3, growth-coupled recombinant acetoin production. Growth reported as OD600 profile (Fig. 4A) but no numeric mu/doubling time or biomass-specific gas rates given in main text. MEASURED specific acetoin titer 0.296 +/- 0.063 mmol/L/OD600. One production-strain replicate reached final OD600 1.72 vs 0.93/0.95 for the other replicate and control. H2/CO2 uptake and CH4 production monitored but quantified only in figures/supplement.

up.method: headspace pressure drop monitored, refilled on depletion; not converted to a numeric per-biomass rate

sec.method: HPLC; growth-coupled Phase 1 volumetric rate (68 h post-induction, 0.259 mM); Phase 2 rate 0.780 umol/L/h to final 0.447 mM

growth_records.cite: Baur, Tina et al. Metabolic engineering of Methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus DeltaH for recombinant acetoin production. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2026.e00275. [PMC13049647]
growth_records.snippet: the induced M. thermautotrophicus [pMVS1111a_Ptet_alsSD] had produced 0.259 +/- 0.033 mM acetoin (Phase 1, 3.814 +/- 0.478 umol L-1 h-1) ... final titer of 0.447 +/- 0.082 mM acetoin (Phase 2, 0.780 +/- 0.216 umol L-1 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Closed-batch 1000-mL bottle with full headspace exchange, 42 C. Growth-coupled Phase 1 acetoin secretion rate 3.814 +/- 0.478 umol/L/h; after growth ceased, Phase 2 (non-growth) rate fell to 0.780 +/- 0.216 umol/L/h; ~2.4-fold higher final titer than controls. No numeric growth rate/doubling time reported (OD600 profile only). H2/CO2 consumption tracked via headspace pressure, not converted to per-biomass rate.

growth_records.cite: Casini et al. An integrated systems biology approach reveals differences in formate metabolism in the genus Methanothermobacter. iScience (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.isci.2023.108016. [PMC10579436]
growth_records.snippet: First Fermentation Experiment - Period 2 (H2 and CO2) delta-H: H2 Uptake 234.36 +/- 40.76; CO2 Uptake 58.18 +/- 11.49; CH4 Production 52.50 +/- 8.53 mmol/gCDW/h (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Strain delta H = M. thermautotrophicus DH. Chemostat: mu = D at steady state. Period 2 D = 1.11 d-1 => mu = 1.11/24 = 0.04625 h-1; doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.0463 = 14.97 h. Uptake/secretion rates read verbatim from Table 2 (mean +/- 1 SD, quadruplicate). pH setpoint 7.3 (inoculation), 65 C. Rates in original units mmol/gCDW/h.

growth_records.cite: Casini et al. An integrated systems biology approach reveals differences in formate metabolism in the genus Methanothermobacter. iScience (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.isci.2023.108016. [PMC10579436]
growth_records.snippet: First Fermentation Experiment - Period 2 (H2 and CO2) ZZ (Z-245): H2 Uptake 147.24 +/- 23.88; CO2 Uptake 34.36 +/- 6.98; CH4 Production 32.34 +/- 3.92 mmol/gCDW/h (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Strain ZZ = M. thermautotrophicus Z-245. mu = D = 1.11 d-1 = 0.0463 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.0463 = 14.97 h. Rates verbatim from Table 2 (mean +/- 1 SD).

growth_records.cite: Casini et al. An integrated systems biology approach reveals differences in formate metabolism in the genus Methanothermobacter. iScience (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.isci.2023.108016. [PMC10579436]
growth_records.snippet: Second Fermentation Experiment (H2 and CO2) delta-HH: H2 Uptake 127.31 +/- 28.18; CO2 Uptake 29.16 +/- 3.91; CH4 Production 30.53 +/- 3.97 mmol/gCDW/h (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Column delta-HH = M. thermautotrophicus DH pMVS1111A empty-vector control on H2/CO2 (second experiment). Second-experiment dilution rate ~1.00 d-1 => mu = 1.00/24 = 0.04167 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.04167 = 16.63 h. Rates verbatim from Table 2.

growth_records.strain: delta H pMVS1111A:PhmtB-fdh (Z-245 fdh cassette), formate-grown
growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with 355 mM sodium formate

up.method: derived from methanogenesis stoichiometry (4 HCOO- -> CH4 + 3 CO2)

growth_records.cite: Casini et al. An integrated systems biology approach reveals differences in formate metabolism in the genus Methanothermobacter. iScience (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.isci.2023.108016. [PMC10579436]
growth_records.snippet: Second Fermentation Experiment (Formate) delta-HF: H2 Production 0.84 +/- 0.34; CO2 Production 31.87 +/- 11.12; CH4 Production 9.69 +/- 3.62 mmol/gCDW/h; fed 355 +/- 5 mM sodium formate (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Column delta-HF = M. thermautotrophicus DH carrying pMVS1111A:PhmtB-fdh(Z-245) grown on formate. On formate the strain PRODUCES (excretes) H2 and CO2 rather than consuming them; these are secretion rows. mu = D ~1.00 d-1 = 0.0417 h-1; doubling 16.63 h. Formate uptake NOT tabulated but paper states formate consumption measured (Fig 3B) once all provided formate consumed; DERIVED here from stoichiometry 4 HCOO- -> CH4 + 3 CO2: formate uptake = 4 x CH4 = 4 x 9.69 = 38.76 mmol/gCDW/h (check: 3 x 9.69 = 29.1 CO2 vs measured 31.87, consistent). Feed 355 mM formate; final medium pH 5.85.

growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with 355 mM sodium formate

up.method: derived from methanogenesis stoichiometry (4 HCOO- -> CH4 + 3 CO2)

growth_records.cite: Casini et al. An integrated systems biology approach reveals differences in formate metabolism in the genus Methanothermobacter. iScience (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.isci.2023.108016. [PMC10579436]
growth_records.snippet: Second Fermentation Experiment (Formate) ZZF: H2 Production 0.55 +/- 0.28; CO2 Production 34.29 +/- 9.77; CH4 Production 10.55 +/- 3.18 mmol/gCDW/h; fed 355 +/- 5 mM sodium formate (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Column ZZF = M. thermautotrophicus Z-245 grown on formate (native fdh). Produces (excretes) H2 and CO2. mu = D ~1.00 d-1 = 0.0417 h-1; doubling 16.63 h. Formate uptake DERIVED from stoichiometry 4 HCOO- -> CH4 + 3 CO2: 4 x 10.55 = 42.20 mmol/gCDW/h (check 3 x 10.55 = 31.7 CO2 vs measured 34.29). Feed 355 mM formate; final medium pH 5.85.
